title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
content:
## Cross-Site Scripting Overview Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) allows attackers to inject client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users. It ranked #7 in OWASP Top 10 2021. XSS can lead to: - Session hijacking - Account takeover - Defacement - Malware distribution - Phishing ## Types of XSS ### 1. Reflected XSS The script is reflected off the server in the response: ``` https://example.com/search?q= ``` **Vulnerable Code:** ```python # Flask - Reflected XSS @app.route('/search') def search(): query = request.args.get('q', '') # Directly inserting user input into HTML return f'
Results for: {query}
' ``` ```javascript // Node.js - Reflected XSS app.get('/search', (req, res) => { const query = req.query.q; res.send(`
Results for: ${query}
`); // DANGEROUS }); ``` **Secure Code:** ```python # Flask - Using escape def search(): from markupsafe import escape query = escape(request.args.get('q', '')) return f'
Results for: {query}
' # Using template auto-escaping (Jinja2) @app.route('/search') def search(): query = request.args.get('q', '') return render_template('search.html', query=query) # Auto-escaped ``` ### 2. Stored XSS The script is permanently stored on the target server: ```python # VULNERABLE - Storing unsanitized input def create_comment(): comment = Comment( text=request.form['text'], # No sanitization user_id=current_user.id ) db.session.add(comment) db.session.commit() return redirect('/comments') # When displayed, script executes in other users' browsers # SECURE - Sanitize before storing def create_comment(): from bleach import clean # Whitelist allowed HTML tags allowed_tags = ['p', 'br', 'strong', 'em'] sanitized_text = clean( request.form['text'], tags=allowed_tags, strip=True ) comment = Comment(text=sanitized_text, user_id=current_user.id) db.session.add(comment) db.session.commit() return redirect('/comments') ``` ### 3. DOM-Based XSS The vulnerability exists in client-side JavaScript: ```html ``` ```javascript // VULNERABLE - eval with user input eval(window.location.hash.substring(1)); // VULNERABLE - document.write document.write('
' + location.search.substring(1) + '
'); // SECURE - Safe alternatives var name = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get('name'); document.getElementById('output').textContent = name; ``` ## Content Security Policy (CSP) CSP headers restrict which resources can be loaded: ### Basic CSP ```python # Flask - CSP headers @app.after_request def set_csp(response): response.headers['Content-Security-Policy'] = ( "default-src 'self'; " "script-src 'self'; " "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; " "img-src 'self' data: https:; " "font-src 'self'; " "connect-src 'self'; " "frame-ancestors 'none'; " "base-uri 'self'; " "form-action 'self'" ) return response ``` ### Advanced CSP ```nginx # Nginx CSP configuration add_header Content-Security-Policy " default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-${request_id}'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-${request_id}'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; media-src 'self'; frame-src 'none'; sandbox allow-forms allow-scripts; report-uri /csp-report; report-to csp-endpoint; " always; ``` ### Nonce-Based CSP ```python # Flask - Nonce-based CSP def generate_nonce(): import secrets return secrets.token_urlsafe(16) @app.route('/') def index(): nonce = generate_nonce() return render_template('index.html', csp_nonce=nonce) # Template: # ``` ## Output Encoding ### HTML Context ```python # Python - HTML escaping from markupsafe import escape safe_text = escape('') # Output: <script>alert(1)</script> ``` ### JavaScript Context ```javascript // JavaScript - Encode for JS context function encodeForJS(str) { return str.replace(/[\\'"\n\r\t\x00-\x1f]/g, (char) => { const escapes = { '\\': '\\\\', '\'': '\\\'', '"': '\\"', '\n': '\\n', '\r': '\\r', '\t': '\\t' }; return escapes[char] || '\\u' + char.charCodeAt(0).toString(16).padStart(4, '0'); }); } // Usage var userInput = ''; document.getElementById('output').innerText = userInput; // Safe ``` ### URL Context ```python # Python - URL encoding from urllib.parse import quote safe_url = quote(user_input, safe='') # Special characters are encoded ``` ### CSS Context ```python # Python - CSS encoding def encode_for_css(str): import re return re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9]', lambda m: f'\\{ord(m.group()):06x}', str) ``` ## HttpOnly Cookies Prevent JavaScript access to session cookies: ```python # Flask - Secure session configuration app.config.update( SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True, # No JavaScript access SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True, # HTTPS only SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE='Lax', # CSRF protection SESSION_COOKIE_NAME='__Secure-session', PERMANENT_SESSION_LIFETIME=1800 ) # Set-Cookie header: # Set-Cookie: session=abc123; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax; Path=/ ``` ```javascript // JavaScript - Cannot access HttpOnly cookies document.cookie // Does NOT include HttpOnly cookies // Only sent with HTTP requests, not accessible via JavaScript fetch('/api/data', { credentials: 'include' // Cookies sent automatically }); ``` ## DOM Sanitization ```javascript // Using DOMPurify for HTML sanitization import DOMPurify from 'dompurify'; const userHTML = ''; const safeHTML = DOMPurify.sanitize(userHTML); // Output: ' document.getElementById('output').innerHTML = safeHTML; ``` ```python # Python - bleach sanitization import bleach # Whitelist specific tags and attributes allowed_tags = ['p', 'br', 'strong', 'em', 'a', 'ul', 'ol', 'li'] allowed_attrs = {'a': ['href', 'title']} def sanitize_html(html): return bleach.clean( html, tags=allowed_tags, attributes=allowed_attrs, strip=True ) ``` ## XSS Prevention Checklist 1. **Use Context-Aware Encoding** - HTML entity encoding for HTML context - JavaScript encoding for JS context - URL encoding for URL context - CSS encoding for CSS context 2. **Use Template Auto-Escaping** - Jinja2, Twig, Handlebars auto-escape by default - Never disable auto-escaping 3. **Implement CSP** - Use nonce-based or hash-based CSP - Report CSP violations 4. **Sanitize HTML Input** - Use DOMPurify (JavaScript) - Use bleach (Python) - Whitelist allowed tags 5. **Use HttpOnly Cookies** - Prevent JavaScript access to session tokens 6. **Avoid危险 Functions** - Don't use innerHTML, document.write, eval - Use textContent, createElement, JSON.parse ## Key Takeaways - Never insert untrusted data into HTML - Use context-aware output encoding - Implement Content Security Policy - Sanitize HTML input with trusted libraries - Use HttpOnly cookies for session management - Test for all three types of XSS - Use automated scanning tools

quiz.question: What is the difference between reflected and stored XSS?
options: A) Reflected is more dangerous than stored
B) Reflected requires user interaction, stored persists on the server
C) Stored only affects mobile users
quiz.answer: B) Reflected requires user interaction, stored persists on the server
quiz.explanation: Reflected XSS requires victim to click a malicious link, while stored XSS is permanently stored and affects all users who view the content.

quiz.question: What does the Content Security Policy (CSP) header do?
options: B) Restricts which resources can be loaded on a page
quiz.answer: B) Restricts which resources can be loaded on a page
quiz.explanation: CSP defines which sources are allowed to load scripts, styles, images, and other resources, preventing XSS attacks.

quiz.question: Which HTML attribute prevents JavaScript access to cookies?
quiz.explanation: The HttpOnly flag prevents JavaScript from accessing cookies, protecting session tokens from XSS-based theft.
